fault_hypothesis: The expiry condition on line 70 uses strict greater-than (`now > adjusted_deadline`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`now >= adjusted_deadline`), so an entry accessed exactly at its deadline is incorrectly treated as still valid.

constraints.intent: When now equals adjusted_deadline the condition should be true (entry expired), but the strict > misses the equality case.
After computing adjusted_deadline, if now equals it the next branch must treat the entry as expired (now >= adjusted_deadline__1 should trigger expiry).
Immediately before using adjusted_deadline in the comparison, if now equals it the expiry condition (>=) should hold.
In the else-branch (entry not expired), now must be strictly less than adjusted_deadline; equality should have taken the then-branch.
